concept: Habitat / Place and Conditions Supporting an Organism
description: The place and relevant conditions in which an organism, population, or source-defined living group dwells and carries out part or all of its life. Habitat can include food, shelter, water, material, seasonal, and relational conditions and may be suitable in some respects but not others. The word does not mean every place where an organism is observed, a permanent home, an untouched area, legal protection, ownership, or proof of habitat quality.
sound_symbolism: The dwelling echo of 'menu' settles into relational 'lora', joining place with the conditions that make continued life possible. The open ending leaves habitat responsive to season and change.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun commonly modified by the organism or group: 'ha koashira ha teluno menulora nai.' (This grove is a habitat for this amphibian.) Name life stage, season, conditions, and scale where they alter the claim. Distinct from 'lokue' (place), 'womu' (home), 'menui' (dwell), and an ecosystem as a wider analytical whole.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Habitat vocabulary makes the material conditions of other beings visible in decisions about land, infrastructure, work, and restoration.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Situated knowledge attends to where beings feed, shelter, reproduce, rest, and move rather than treating land as empty background.

tags.nature: the place and conditions in which a living group dwells
tags.spatial: a bounded or distributed area understood relative to an organism
